tool.name: chain to bigChain
tool.description: convert UCSC chain alignments to bigChain + bigLink BED for assembly hubs
tool.command: ## Galaxy stores datasets as .dat and serves chain data uncompressed; the script ## switches on the file suffix, so symlink to a real .chain name. ln -s '$chain' in.chain && python '$__tool_directory__/chain_to_bigChain.py' in.chain '$bigchain_bed' '$biglink_bed'

param.label: Chain alignment
param.help: UCSC pairwise alignment chain file. Chains with a non-'+' target strand are skipped with a warning.

tool.help:
**chain to bigChain**
Converts a [UCSC chain](https://genome.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/help/chain.html) pairwise alignment file into the two BED tables required to build a [bigChain](https://genomearchive.ucsc.edu/goldenPath/help/bigChain.html) track for an assembly hub, mirroring the structure produced by `hgLoadChain -noBin`:
- **bigChain.bed** (bed6+6, 12 columns): one row per chain - `chrom, chromStart, chromEnd, name, score(1000), strand, tSize, qName, qSize, qStart, qEnd, chainScore`. - **bigLink.bed** (bed4+1, 5 columns): one row per aligned block - `chrom, chromStart, chromEnd, name(chainId), qStart`.
Block link rows are emitted by walking each chain in target coordinates. Chains whose target strand is not `+` are skipped and reported on stderr.
-----
Feed the resulting BED files (after sorting and `bedToBigBed` with `bigChain.as` / `bigLink.as`) into the hub-building steps of the pangenome pipeline.
Ported from `Pv4-pangenome/v3/tools/chain_to_bigChain.py`.
